packs.title: Everyday verbs IV with aspect pairs

words.note: pf. odvesti (away) / dovesti (here); voditi psa u šetnju = walk the dog

example.target: Svako jutro vodim psa u park.
example.gloss: Every morning I take the dog to the park.

words.note: impf. dovoditi; for things use donijeti

example.target: Sutra ću dovesti sestru na večeru.
example.gloss: Tomorrow I will bring my sister to dinner.

example.target: Možeš li me odvesti na kolodvor?
example.gloss: Can you take me to the station?

example.target: Poštar nam donosi pisma svaki dan.
example.gloss: The postman brings us letters every day.
Don't forget to bring an umbrella!
I'll take the rubbish out.
I receive my salary on the first of the month.
Yesterday I received your message.
I often change my mind.
I want to change jobs.
The neighbour repairs old bicycles.
The repairman fixed the washing machine.
This car keeps breaking down.

example.target: Moram očistiti kupaonicu prije dolaska gostiju.
example.gloss: I have to clean the bathroom before the guests arrive.
Wash your hands before eating!
I dry the laundry on the balcony.
She dried her hair before going out.
On Sundays I iron shirts.
We tidy the flat every Saturday.

example.target: Baci to u smeće, molim te.
example.gloss: Throw that in the rubbish, please.
At the gym I lift weights.
Raise your hand if you know the answer.
In the evening we lower the blinds.
Put the bag down on the floor.
I charge my phone every night.

words.note: impf. puniti; napuniti što čime (+ instrumental)

example.gloss: I filled the bottle with water.
Empty your pockets before washing.
Don't turn on the light, the child is sleeping.
Turn on the TV, the match is starting.
I always turn off the computer before going to bed.
Turn off the light when you leave.
Do you always lock the door?

example.target: Zaključala sam stan i otišla na posao.
example.gloss: I locked the flat and went to work.
Every morning I unlock the shop.

example.target: Ne mogu otključati vrata ovim ključem.
example.gloss: I can't unlock the door with this key.
The teacher patiently explains grammar.

example.target: Možeš li mi prevesti ovu rečenicu?
example.gloss: Can you translate this sentence for me?
Foreigners have a hard time pronouncing Croatian names.

example.target: Ne mogu izgovoriti tu dugu riječ.
example.gloss: I can't pronounce that long word.

words.en: to get in touch; to answer (the phone)
words.note: pf. javiti se; javljati se komu (+ dative)

example.target: Zašto se ne javljaš na telefon?
example.gloss: Why don't you answer the phone?

words.note: pf. iskoristiti; also koristiti se čime (+ instrumental)

example.gloss: I use a dictionary while learning Croatian.
The restaurant offers homemade dishes.
She offered me coffee.
I pay the rent every month.
We buy fruit at the market.
The waiter is opening a bottle of wine.
Who is closing the shop tonight?
Close the window, it's cold.
I help grandma with the garden.
She smelled the scent of fresh bread.

words.en: to look forward to; to rejoice
words.note: pf. obradovati se; radovati se čemu (+ dative)

example.gloss: I'm looking forward to your arrival.

words.en: to worry; to take care of
words.note: pf. zabrinuti se; brinuti se za koga / o komu

example.target: Mama se uvijek brine za nas.
example.gloss: Mum always worries about us.

example.target: Zabrinuo sam se kad se nisi javila.
example.gloss: I got worried when you didn't call.

words.note: pf. naljutiti se; ljutiti se na koga (+ accusative)

example.gloss: Don't be angry with me.
The boss got angry because of the mistake.

words.note: pf. svidjeti se; dative experiencer: sviđa mi se = I like it

example.gloss: I like this city.
I liked the film a lot.

words.note: impf. zaljubljivati se; zaljubiti se u koga (+ accusative)

example.target: Zaljubio se u kolegicu s posla.
example.gloss: He fell in love with a colleague from work.

words.note: pf. posvađati se; svađati se s kim (+ instrumental)

example.target: Braća se često svađaju oko igračaka.
example.gloss: The brothers often argue over toys.
She had a fight with her best friend.

words.note: pf. pomiriti se; miriti se s kim (+ instrumental)

example.target: Uvijek se brzo mirimo nakon svađe.
example.gloss: We always make up quickly after an argument.

example.target: Pomirili su se nakon dva dana.
example.gloss: They made up after two days.

words.note: pf. oženiti se; oženiti se kim (+ instrumental)

example.target: Moj se brat ženi u lipnju.
example.gloss: My brother is getting married in June.
He got married last year.

words.note: pf. udati se; udati se za koga (+ accusative)

example.gloss: My sister is getting married next month.
She married her neighbour.

words.note: impf. rastajati se; rastati se od koga (+ genitive)

example.target: Rastali su se nakon deset godina braka.
example.gloss: They separated after ten years of marriage.

words.note: pf. naviknuti se; navikavati se na što (+ accusative)

example.target: Polako se navikavam na novi posao.
example.gloss: I'm slowly getting used to the new job.

words.note: impf. navikavati se; naviknuti se na što (+ accusative)

example.target: Brzo sam se naviknuo na život u Zagrebu.
example.gloss: I quickly got used to life in Zagreb.

words.note: no perfective partner in this meaning

example.target: Djeca su se danas lijepo ponašala.
example.gloss: The children behaved nicely today.

words.en: to try hard, to make an effort

example.target: Trudim se govoriti hrvatski svaki dan.
example.gloss: I try to speak Croatian every day.

words.note: impf. truditi se; potruditi se oko čega (+ genitive)

example.target: Potrudila se oko večere za goste.
example.gloss: She made an effort with the dinner for the guests.
I'm joking, don't be angry!

words.note: no common perfective; compare dosadno mi je = I'm bored

example.target: Djeca se dosađuju kad pada kiša.
example.gloss: The children get bored when it rains.

example.target: Na zabavi smo se odlično zabavljali.
example.gloss: We had a great time at the party.

example.target: Idemo van da se malo zabavimo.
example.gloss: Let's go out to have some fun.
On weekends I relax with music.

example.target: Opusti se, sve će biti u redu.
example.gloss: Relax, everything will be fine.

words.note: impf. umarati se; umoriti se od čega (+ genitive)

example.target: Umorila sam se od dugog hodanja.
example.gloss: I got tired from the long walk.

words.en: to rest, to take a rest

example.gloss: Sit down and rest a bit.

words.note: pf. začuditi se; čuditi se čemu (+ dative)

example.target: Čudim se što još nisi umoran.
example.gloss: I'm surprised you're not tired yet.

words.note: pf. posramiti se; sramiti se čega (+ genitive)

example.gloss: Don't be ashamed of your mistakes.
Calm down and breathe deeply.
She got upset about the bad grade.

words.note: pf. uplašiti se; plašiti se čega (+ genitive); synonym: bojati se

example.gloss: The child is afraid of the dark.

words.note: impf. plašiti se; uplašiti se čega (+ genitive)

example.gloss: I got scared by the loud noise.

words.en: to laugh, to burst out laughing
words.note: impf. smijati se; nasmijati se čemu (+ dative)

example.target: Svi su se nasmijali njegovoj šali.
example.gloss: Everyone laughed at his joke.
The baby started crying in the middle of the night.
Grandma hugs her grandchildren when saying goodbye.
She hugged me tightly at the station.
He kissed her on the cheek.

words.en: to meet, to get to know
words.note: impf. upoznavati; upoznati se s kim (+ instrumental)

example.target: Drago mi je što sam te upoznao.
example.gloss: I'm glad I met you.

words.note: impf. zaručivati se; zaručiti se s kim (+ instrumental)

example.gloss: They got engaged on their summer holiday.
She is very kind.

example.target: Jako mi se sviđa ovaj film.
example.gloss: I like this film a lot.
The test was quite hard.
I drank too much coffee.
You sleep too little, only five hours.
We have enough time until the train.
I almost forgot your birthday.

example.target: Skoro svaki dan idem pješice na posao.
example.gloss: Almost every day I walk to work.
I completely forgot about the meeting.
That's completely normal.

words.note: usually with negation: uopće ne = not at all

example.gloss: I don't understand this question at all.

example.target: Stvarno ne znam gdje je ključ.
example.gloss: I really don't know where the key is.
The dinner was truly delicious.
He will surely come on time.
We'll probably be late for the train.
Unfortunately, we have no more tickets.

words.note: instrumental of sreća used as an adverb

example.gloss: Luckily, I had an umbrella.
Of course I can help you.
Actually, I don't like coffee.

example.target: Bilo je kasno, ali ipak smo došli.
example.gloss: It was late, but we came anyway.

words.note: connector between sentences, more formal than ali

example.target: Htjeli smo na plažu; međutim, počela je kiša.
example.gloss: We wanted to go to the beach; however, it started to rain.
Hurry up, otherwise we'll be late.
Drink at least a glass of water.

example.target: Čak i djeca razumiju ovo pravilo.
example.gloss: Even children understand this rule.
I've just arrived home.
Suddenly heavy rain started.

words.note: also as interjection: samo polako! = take it easy!

example.gloss: Speak slowly, please.
Children learn languages quickly.

example.target: Ne pričaj tako glasno, beba spava.
example.gloss: Don't talk so loudly, the baby is sleeping.
Enter the room quietly.
Read the instructions carefully.

example.target: Vlak je stigao točno u podne.
example.gloss: The train arrived exactly at noon.
I wrote your name wrong.
I accidentally broke a glass.
He didn't do it on purpose.
Shall we go to lunch together?

words.note: sama (f.), samo (n.); not the same as sam in ja sam (I am)

example.gloss: I live alone in a small flat.
Can we pay separately?

example.target: Rado ću ti pomoći oko selidbe.
example.gloss: I'll gladly help you with the move.
Grandpa has difficulty walking up the stairs.
It's easy to remember this song.
I can't wait for the summer holiday!
The trip takes approximately two hours.
On weekends we mostly rest.
I love fruit, especially strawberries.
While I cook, I listen to the radio.

example.target: Nazvat ću te čim stignem kući.
example.gloss: I'll call you as soon as I get home.

words.note: introduces a clause; compare prije + genitive

example.target: Operi ruke prije nego što jedeš.
example.gloss: Wash your hands before you eat.

words.note: introduces a clause; compare nakon + genitive

example.target: Nakon što smo večerali, gledali smo film.
example.gloss: After we had dinner, we watched a film.

example.target: Iako pada kiša, idemo u šetnju.
example.gloss: Although it's raining, we're going for a walk.
Although he is tired, he is still working.

words.note: gives a reason; more formal than jer

example.target: Budući da nemam auto, idem vlakom.
example.gloss: Since I don't have a car, I'm going by train.

example.target: Učim hrvatski zato što volim Hrvatsku.
example.gloss: I'm learning Croatian because I love Croatia.

example.target: Kasnio je autobus, tako da sam zakasnio na posao.
example.gloss: The bus was late, so I was late for work.

words.note: bi changes with the subject: kako bih, kako bismo...

example.target: Štedim novac kako bih kupio stan.
example.gloss: I'm saving money in order to buy a flat.
Instead of studying, you're watching TV.
Everyone came except Marko.

example.target: Stan je lijep; osim toga, blizu je centra.
example.gloss: The flat is nice; besides, it's close to the centre.

example.target: Karta je skupa, dakle idemo pješice.
example.gloss: The ticket is expensive, so we're walking.
It's snowing, therefore drive slowly.
First we have breakfast, then we go out.

example.target: Pročitaj tekst, potom odgovori na pitanja.
example.gloss: Read the text, then answer the questions.

example.target: Ne mogu doći; naime, radim u subotu.
example.gloss: I can't come; you see, I work on Saturday.

example.target: Volim sport, primjerice plivanje i tenis.
example.gloss: I like sport, for instance swimming and tennis.

example.target: Jedem puno voća, na primjer jabuke i banane.
example.gloss: I eat a lot of fruit, for example apples and bananas.

example.target: S jedne strane volim grad, s druge mi nedostaje priroda.
example.gloss: On the one hand I love the city, on the other I miss nature.

example.target: Posao je težak; s druge strane, plaća je dobra.
example.gloss: The job is hard; on the other hand, the pay is good.
First we have to buy the tickets.

example.target: Prvo, nemam vremena; drugo, nemam novca.
example.gloss: First, I have no time; second, I have no money.

example.target: Na kraju smo ipak našli hotel.
example.gloss: In the end we found a hotel after all.
In short, the trip was excellent.

example.target: Dolazim za tjedan dana, to jest u petak.
example.gloss: I'm coming in a week, that is, on Friday.

words.note: verb is negated too: ne pijem ni... ni...

example.target: Ne pijem ni kavu ni čaj.
example.gloss: I drink neither coffee nor tea.

example.target: Ili idemo sada ili ćemo zakasniti.
example.gloss: Either we go now or we'll be late.
She speaks both English and German.

example.target: Što se tiče cijene, sve je u redu.
example.gloss: As far as the price is concerned, everything is fine.

example.target: Zovem u vezi s oglasom za stan.
example.gloss: I'm calling about the advertisement for the flat.

example.target: Zbog kiše nismo išli na izlet.
example.gloss: Because of the rain we didn't go on the trip.

example.target: Nisam spavao i zbog toga sam umoran.
example.gloss: I didn't sleep and that's why I'm tired.
Despite the rain, the match was played.

words.note: contrasts two statements; softer than ali

example.target: Ja pijem kavu, a on pije čaj.
example.gloss: I drink coffee, and he drinks tea.

words.note: after negation: ne X, nego Y; also in comparisons

example.gloss: It's not blue but green.
I also love the sea.
It's cold like in winter.

example.target: Sve je prošlo kao što smo planirali.
example.gloss: Everything went as we planned.

example.target: Idemo na izlet bez obzira na vrijeme.
example.gloss: We're going on the trip regardless of the weather.

example.target: U svakom slučaju, javit ću ti se sutra.
example.gloss: In any case, I'll get in touch with you tomorrow.

example.target: Prije svega, hvala vam na pomoći.
example.gloss: First of all, thank you for your help.

example.target: Otkad živim ovdje, osjećam se bolje.
example.gloss: Since I've been living here, I feel better.

example.target: Kasno je; drugim riječima, moramo ići.
example.gloss: It's late; in other words, we have to go.
